<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>Codex setup system analysis</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis snapshot</strong>: <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code> — configuration system.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Codex's configuration is not a single file. A 7-layer model is operated, and each layer is recursively merged in TOML format. The key is how to resolve priority conflicts between layers, per-key origin tracking, and policy restrictions based on requirements.</p>
<hr>
<h2>1. Layer architecture</h2>
<p>Layer priorities (higher down):</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>System</strong> — <code>/etc/codex/config.toml</code> (Unix) or <code>%ProgramData%\OpenAI\Codex\config.toml</code> (Windows)</li>
<li><strong>User</strong> — <code>$CODEX_HOME/config.toml</code></li>
<li><strong>Project</strong> — CWD standard <code>.codex/config.toml</code> (tree walk + repo root, disabled when untrusted)</li>
<li><strong>Session flags</strong> — CLI <code>--config</code> override (dotted-path TOML write)</li>
<li><strong>Legacy managed config</strong> — <code>managed_config.toml</code> (file or MDM)</li>
<li><strong>MDM managed config</strong> — macOS managed preferences (based on <code>defaults</code>, macOS only)</li>
</ol>
<p>Separately, the <strong>Requirements</strong> channel operates independently of the config layer:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Cloud requirements</strong> — Remotely managed constraints</li>
<li><strong>MDM requirements</strong> — Restrictions based on macOS managed preferences</li>
<li><strong>System requirements</strong> — <code>/etc/codex/requirements.toml</code></li>
</ul>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph "Config Layers (낮→높 우선순위)"
        SYS["① System config.toml"]
        USR["② User config.toml"]
        PRJ["③ Project .codex/config.toml"]
        CLI["④ Session flags (CLI --config)"]
        MCF["⑤ Legacy managed_config.toml"]
        MDM["⑥ MDM managed config (macOS)"]
    end

    subgraph "Requirements (정책 제약)"
        CRQ["Cloud requirements"]
        MRQ["MDM requirements"]
        SRQ["System requirements.toml"]
    end

    SYS --> STACK["ConfigLayerStack"]
    USR --> STACK
    PRJ --> STACK
    CLI --> STACK
    MCF --> STACK
    MDM --> STACK

    CRQ --> REQ["ConfigRequirements"]
    MRQ --> REQ
    SRQ --> REQ

    STACK --> MERGE["effective_config()\nTOML 재귀 병합"]
    STACK --> ORIG["per-key origins()"]
    REQ --> STACK

    MERGE --> CFG["Config 최종 구조체"]
    REQ --> CFG
</code></pre>
<h3>1.1 Layer loading flow</h3>
<p><code>load_config_layers_state()</code> in <code>config_loader/mod.rs</code> assembles the entire layer stack:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config_loader/mod.rs — 진입점
pub async fn load_config_layers_state(
    codex_home: &#x26;Path,
    cwd: Option&#x3C;AbsolutePathBuf>,
    cli_overrides: &#x26;[(String, TomlValue)],
    overrides: LoaderOverrides,
    cloud_requirements: CloudRequirementsLoader,
) -> io::Result&#x3C;ConfigLayerStack> {
    // 1) requirements 수집: cloud → MDM → system requirements.toml
    let mut config_requirements_toml = ConfigRequirementsWithSources::default();
    if let Some(requirements) = cloud_requirements.get().await...? {
        config_requirements_toml.merge_unset_fields(
            RequirementSource::CloudRequirements, requirements);
    }
    // macOS MDM requirements
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    macos::load_managed_admin_requirements_toml(&#x26;mut config_requirements_toml, ...);
    // /etc/codex/requirements.toml
    load_requirements_toml(&#x26;mut config_requirements_toml, system_requirements_toml_file()?);

    // 2) config 레이어 조립: system → user → project → session flags → legacy managed
    let mut layers = Vec::&#x3C;ConfigLayerEntry>::new();
    layers.push(/* system */);
    layers.push(/* user */);
    // project 레이어: cwd에서 .codex/ 폴더를 tree-walk로 검색
    if let Some(cwd) = cwd {
        let project_layers = load_project_layers(&#x26;cwd, ...);
        layers.extend(project_layers);
    }
    if let Some(cli_overrides_layer) = cli_overrides_layer {
        layers.push(ConfigLayerEntry::new(ConfigLayerSource::SessionFlags, ...));
    }
    // legacy managed_config.toml (파일 + MDM)
    ...

    // 3) ConfigLayerStack 구성 — layers + requirements → 최종 스택
    ConfigLayerStack::new(layers, config_requirements_toml.try_into()?, ...)
}
</code></pre>
<h3>1.2 Config final build</h3>
<p><code>ConfigBuilder</code> builds the final <code>Config</code> structure from the layer stack:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/mod.rs — ConfigBuilder::build()
pub async fn build(self) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;Config> {
    let config_layer_stack = load_config_layers_state(
        &#x26;codex_home, Some(cwd), &#x26;cli_overrides, loader_overrides, cloud_requirements,
    ).await?;

    // effective_config(): disabled 레이어 제외, 재귀 TOML 병합
    let merged_toml = config_layer_stack.effective_config();

    // TOML Value → ConfigToml 역직렬화
    let config_toml: ConfigToml = merged_toml.try_into()?;

    // ConfigToml + ConfigOverrides + requirements → 최종 Config
    Config::load_config_with_layer_stack(config_toml, harness_overrides, codex_home, config_layer_stack)
}
</code></pre>
<h3>1.3 Requirements apply</h3>
<p>Inside <code>Config::load_config_with_layer_stack()</code> the requirements are applied to each field:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/mod.rs — load_config_with_layer_stack()
let ConfigRequirements {
    approval_policy: mut constrained_approval_policy,
    sandbox_policy: mut constrained_sandbox_policy,
    web_search_mode: mut constrained_web_search_mode,
    feature_requirements,
    mcp_servers,
    exec_policy: _,
    enforce_residency,
    network: network_requirements,
} = config_layer_stack.requirements().clone();

// 사용자 설정값이 requirement 제약에 위반되면 fallback 적용 + 경고 발행
apply_requirement_constrained_value(
    "approval_policy", configured_value,
    &#x26;mut constrained_approval_policy, &#x26;mut startup_warnings)?;
</code></pre>
<p><code>apply_requirement_constrained_value</code> automatically falls back to a safe value and collects a warning when a constraint is violated:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">fn apply_requirement_constrained_value&#x3C;T>(
    field_name: &#x26;'static str,
    configured_value: T,
    constrained_value: &#x26;mut ConstrainedWithSource&#x3C;T>,
    startup_warnings: &#x26;mut Vec&#x3C;String>,
) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;()> {
    if let Err(err) = constrained_value.set(configured_value) {
        let fallback_value = constrained_value.get().clone();
        startup_warnings.push(format!(
            "Configured value for `{field_name}` is disallowed by requirements; \
             falling back to required value {fallback_value:?}. Details: {err}"
        ));
        constrained_value.set(fallback_value)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>2. Permission system</h2>
<p><code>config/permissions.rs</code> compiles TOML-based permission profiles. Create a sandbox policy with two axes: file system and network.</p>
<h3>2.1 Permission TOML structure</h3>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/permissions.rs — 핵심 타입
pub struct PermissionsToml {
    pub entries: BTreeMap&#x3C;String, PermissionProfileToml>,  // 이름별 프로파일
}

pub struct PermissionProfileToml {
    pub filesystem: Option&#x3C;FilesystemPermissionsToml>,  // 파일시스템 규칙
    pub network: Option&#x3C;NetworkToml>,                    // 네트워크 규칙
}

// 파일시스템 경로별 접근 권한
pub enum FilesystemPermissionToml {
    Access(FileSystemAccessMode),                        // 단순: read/write/read_write
    Scoped(BTreeMap&#x3C;String, FileSystemAccessMode>),      // 하위경로별 세분화
}
</code></pre>
<p>Example TOML settings:</p>
<pre><code class="language-toml">[permissions.strict]
[permissions.strict.filesystem]
":project_roots" = "read_write"
":tmpdir" = "read_write"
"/usr/local/bin" = "read"

[permissions.strict.network]
enabled = true
mode = "limited"
allowed_domains = ["api.openai.com", "github.com"]
</code></pre>
<h3>2.2 Permission compilation flow</h3>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    A["config.toml\n[permissions.NAME]"] --> B["resolve_permission_profile()"]
    B --> C["compile_permission_profile()"]
    C --> D["FileSystemSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> E["NetworkSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> W["startup_warnings"]

    subgraph "특수 경로 해석"
        S1[":root → Root"]
        S2[":minimal → Minimal"]
        S3[":project_roots → ProjectRoots"]
        S4[":tmpdir → Tmpdir"]
        S5[":unknown → Unknown + 경고"]
    end
    C --> S1
</code></pre>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/permissions.rs — 퍼미션 컴파일
pub(crate) fn compile_permission_profile(
    permissions: &#x26;PermissionsToml,
    profile_name: &#x26;str,
    startup_warnings: &#x26;mut Vec&#x3C;String>,
) -> io::Result&#x3C;(FileSystemSandboxPolicy, NetworkSandboxPolicy)> {
    let profile = resolve_permission_profile(permissions, profile_name)?;

    // 파일시스템: 각 경로를 FileSystemSandboxEntry로 컴파일
    let mut entries = Vec::new();
    if let Some(filesystem) = profile.filesystem.as_ref() {
        for (path, permission) in &#x26;filesystem.entries {
            compile_filesystem_permission(path, permission, &#x26;mut entries, startup_warnings)?;
        }
    }
    // 네트워크: enabled 여부에 따라 Enabled/Restricted
    let network_sandbox_policy = compile_network_sandbox_policy(profile.network.as_ref());

    Ok((FileSystemSandboxPolicy::restricted(entries), network_sandbox_policy))
}
</code></pre>
<h3>2.3 Special paths and forward compatibility</h3>
<p>Round-trip the unrecognized <code>:special_path</code> to <code>FileSystemSpecialPath::Unknown</code> to ensure that config load does not fail in older versions:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// 전방 호환 파서: 새 특수 경로 추가 시 구버전이 reject하지 않음
fn parse_special_path(path: &#x26;str) -> Option&#x3C;FileSystemSpecialPath> {
    match path {
        ":root"          => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Root),
        ":minimal"       => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Minimal),
        ":project_roots" => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::project_roots(None)),
        ":tmpdir"        => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Tmpdir),
        _ if path.starts_with(':') => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::unknown(path, None)),
        _ => None,
    }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>2.4 NetworkToml details</h3>
<p>Network settings are translated into <code>NetworkProxyConfig</code> to perform proxy-level network control:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">pub struct NetworkToml {
    pub enabled: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    pub proxy_url: Option&#x3C;String>,
    pub enable_socks5: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    pub mode: Option&#x3C;NetworkMode>,               // limited | full
    pub allowed_domains: Option&#x3C;Vec&#x3C;String>>,     // 화이트리스트
    pub denied_domains: Option&#x3C;Vec&#x3C;String>>,      // 블랙리스트
    pub allow_unix_sockets: Option&#x3C;Vec&#x3C;String>>,
    pub dangerously_allow_non_loopback_proxy: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    pub dangerously_allow_all_unix_sockets: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    // ...
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>3. Feature Flag system</h2>
<p><code>config/managed_features.rs</code> manages feature flags through the <code>ManagedFeatures</code> wrapper. Features pinned by requirements cannot be changed by the user.</p>
<h3>3.1 ManagedFeatures structure</h3>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/managed_features.rs
pub struct ManagedFeatures {
    value: ConstrainedWithSource&#x3C;Features>,
    pinned_features: BTreeMap&#x3C;Feature, bool>,  // requirements가 고정한 feature
}

impl ManagedFeatures {
    // requirements에서 pinned feature 파싱 → 후보 정규화 → 검증
    pub(crate) fn from_configured(
        configured_features: Features,
        feature_requirements: Option&#x3C;Sourced&#x3C;FeatureRequirementsToml>>,
    ) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;Self> {
        let (pinned_features, source) = match feature_requirements {
            Some(Sourced { value, source }) => (
                parse_feature_requirements(value, &#x26;source)?, Some(source)
            ),
            None => (BTreeMap::new(), None),
        };
        // pinned feature를 candidate에 강제 적용 후 의존성 정규화
        let normalized = normalize_candidate(configured_features, &#x26;pinned_features);
        validate_pinned_features(&#x26;normalized, &#x26;pinned_features, source.as_ref())?;
        Ok(Self { ... })
    }

    // 변경 시도 시 pinned feature 제약 검증
    pub fn set(&#x26;mut self, candidate: Features) -> ConstraintResult&#x3C;()> {
        let normalized = self.normalize_and_validate(candidate)?;
        self.value.value.set(normalized)
    }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>3.2 Feature normalization flow</h3>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    A["사용자 features\n(config.toml)"] --> B["Features::from_sources()"]
    C["프로파일 features"] --> B
    D["CLI overrides"] --> B
    B --> E["configured_features"]
    F["requirements.toml\nfeature_requirements"] --> G["parse_feature_requirements()"]
    G --> H["pinned_features\nBTreeMap&#x3C;Feature, bool>"]
    E --> I["normalize_candidate()"]
    H --> I
    I --> J["pin 적용 + 의존성 정규화"]
    J --> K["validate_pinned_features()"]
    K --> L["ManagedFeatures"]
</code></pre>
<p>Features are synthesized from multiple sources — global config, profile config, legacy toggle, CLI override:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/mod.rs — Feature 합성
let configured_features = Features::from_sources(
    FeatureConfigSource {
        features: cfg.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: None,
        experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch: cfg.experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch,
        experimental_use_unified_exec_tool: cfg.experimental_use_unified_exec_tool,
    },
    FeatureConfigSource {  // 프로파일 레벨 오버라이드
        features: config_profile.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: config_profile.include_apply_patch_tool,
        ...
    },
    feature_overrides,  // CLI --config 등
);
let features = ManagedFeatures::from_configured(configured_features, feature_requirements)?;
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>4. Model provider management</h2>
<h3>4.1 ModelProviderInfo</h3>
<p>Model providers consist of built-in defaults + custom extensions:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// model_provider_info.rs
pub struct ModelProviderInfo {
    pub name: String,                               // 표시명
    pub base_url: Option&#x3C;String>,                   // API 엔드포인트
    pub env_key: Option&#x3C;String>,                    // API 키 환경변수
    pub wire_api: WireApi,                          // responses (chat는 제거됨)
    pub query_params: Option&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, String>>,
    pub http_headers: Option&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, String>>,
    pub env_http_headers: Option&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, String>>,
    pub request_max_retries: Option&#x3C;u64>,
    pub stream_max_retries: Option&#x3C;u64>,
    pub stream_idle_timeout_ms: Option&#x3C;u64>,
    // ...
}
</code></pre>
<p>Built-in providers:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>openai</code> — OpenAI API (default)</li>
<li><code>ollama</code> — Ollama OSS</li>
<li><code>lmstudio</code> — LM Studio OSS</li>
</ul>
<p>Custom providers can be added to the <code>[model_providers]</code> table, but built-in IDs cannot be overridden:</p>
<pre><code class="language-toml"># config.toml 예시
[model_providers.my-local]
name = "My Local LLM"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
wire_api = "responses"
env_key = "MY_LOCAL_API_KEY"
</code></pre>
<h3>4.2 Profile system</h3>
<p>With <code>ConfigProfile</code> you can convert model + sandbox + feature as one unit:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/profile.rs
pub struct ConfigProfile {
    pub model: Option&#x3C;String>,
    pub model_provider: Option&#x3C;String>,
    pub approval_policy: Option&#x3C;AskForApproval>,
    pub sandbox_mode: Option&#x3C;SandboxMode>,
    pub model_reasoning_effort: Option&#x3C;ReasoningEffort>,
    pub model_reasoning_summary: Option&#x3C;ReasoningSummary>,
    pub model_verbosity: Option&#x3C;Verbosity>,
    pub personality: Option&#x3C;Personality>,
    pub features: Option&#x3C;FeaturesToml>,
    pub tools: Option&#x3C;ToolsToml>,
    pub web_search: Option&#x3C;WebSearchMode>,
    // ...
}
</code></pre>
<pre><code class="language-toml"># config.toml — 프로파일 전환 예시
profile = "fast"

[profiles.fast]
model = "gpt-4.1-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
sandbox_mode = "write"

[profiles.careful]
model = "o3"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
approval_policy = "unless-allow-listed"
sandbox_mode = "locked"
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>5. Config editing system</h2>
<p>The <code>ConfigEdit</code> enum in <code>config/edit.rs</code> defines the atomic unit of configuration change. <code>ConfigEditsBuilder</code> updates <code>config.toml</code> with an atomic write:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/edit.rs — 지원되는 편집 연산
pub enum ConfigEdit {
    SetModel { model: Option&#x3C;String>, effort: Option&#x3C;ReasoningEffort> },
    SetServiceTier { service_tier: Option&#x3C;ServiceTier> },
    SetModelPersonality { personality: Option&#x3C;Personality> },
    ReplaceMcpServers(BTreeMap&#x3C;String, McpServerConfig>),
    SetSkillConfig { path: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    SetProjectTrustLevel { path: PathBuf, level: TrustLevel },
    SetPath { segments: Vec&#x3C;String>, value: TomlItem },     // 임의 dotted-path 설정
    ClearPath { segments: Vec&#x3C;String> },                     // 임의 dotted-path 삭제
    // 각종 notice/flag 토글...
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>6. Agent role system</h2>
<p><code>config/agent_roles.rs</code> merges agent role definitions for each layer. Roles are loaded in two paths: TOML declaration + auto-discovery of <code>agents/</code> directory:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/agent_roles.rs — 레이어별 역할 병합
pub(crate) fn load_agent_roles(
    cfg: &#x26;ConfigToml,
    config_layer_stack: &#x26;ConfigLayerStack,
    startup_warnings: &#x26;mut Vec&#x3C;String>,
) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;BTreeMap&#x3C;String, AgentRoleConfig>> {
    // 낮은 우선순위 레이어부터 순회하며 역할 수집
    let layers = config_layer_stack.get_layers(
        ConfigLayerStackOrdering::LowestPrecedenceFirst, false);
    let mut roles: BTreeMap&#x3C;String, AgentRoleConfig> = BTreeMap::new();
    for layer in layers {
        // 1) [agents.roles] 선언 파싱
        // 2) agents/ 디렉토리에서 .toml 파일 자동 발견
        // 3) 같은 이름 → 상위 레이어가 하위 필드 merge
    }
    Ok(roles)
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>7. ConfigService — Runtime Configuration API</h2>
<p><code>ConfigService</code> in <code>config/service.rs</code> provides CRUD of settings through app-server. Supports layer metadata query, per-key override status, and batch write:</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    APP["App Server / TUI"] --> SVC["ConfigService"]
    SVC --> READ["ConfigReadParams\n레이어 메타 + per-key origin"]
    SVC --> WRITE["ConfigValueWriteParams\ndotted-path 단일 쓰기"]
    SVC --> BATCH["ConfigBatchWriteParams\n다중 키 atomic 쓰기"]
    SVC --> TOML["config.toml\natomic file write"]
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>8. JSON Schema creation</h2>
<p><code>config/schema.rs</code> automatically creates Draft-07 JSON Schema from <code>ConfigToml</code>. The <code>features</code> and <code>mcp_servers</code> keys are treated as custom schemas:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/schema.rs
pub fn config_schema() -> RootSchema {
    SchemaSettings::draft07()
        .with(|settings| { settings.option_add_null_type = false; })
        .into_generator()
        .into_root_schema_for::&#x3C;ConfigToml>()
}

// features 키: 알려진 feature + legacy key만 허용, additional_properties = false
pub(crate) fn features_schema(schema_gen: &#x26;mut SchemaGenerator) -> Schema {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        validation.properties.insert(feature.key.to_string(), ...);
    }
    validation.additional_properties = Some(Box::new(Schema::Bool(false)));
    ...
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>9. ConfigToml main field map</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph "ConfigToml 주요 섹션"
        M["model / model_provider\n모델 선택"]
        P["permissions\n파일시스템·네트워크 샌드박스"]
        F["features\n기능 플래그"]
        MCP["mcp_servers\nMCP 서버 정의"]
        PRF["profiles\n명명된 프로파일"]
        AG["agents\n에이전트 역할·스레드 제한"]
        MEM["memories\n메모리 서브시스템"]
        SK["skills\n스킬 토글"]
        PL["plugins\n플러그인 설정"]
        PRJ["projects\n프로젝트별 trust_level"]
        TUI["tui\nUI 설정 (테마, 상태줄 등)"]
    end
</code></pre>
<hr>

<h3>Fork Perspective</h3>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> 7-layer layering and TOML merge are good for reuse</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> Per-key origin tracking can be used directly in the settings debugging UI</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>ConfigRequirements</code> is the core of policy application, so it must be taken along with exec-policy and sandbox.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> The pinned + normalize pattern of <code>ManagedFeatures</code> is suitable for enterprise feature gates.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>ConfigEdit</code> + atomic write is a safe configuration change pattern in a concurrent environment.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> permission profile + special path system is closely linked to the sandbox.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> The MDM layer is exclusive to macOS, so the scope must be determined when forking.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> cloud requirements (<code>cloud_requirements.rs</code>) is OpenAI dependent and therefore subject to removal</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> The built-in provider list in <code>model_provider_info.rs</code> needs to be replaced when forking.</li>
</ul>

<h2>Korean corpus (reference)</h2>
<blockquote>
<p>Verbatim KO corpus <code>008_config-system/08_co_config.md</code> for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<h1>Codex 설정 시스템 분석</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code> — configuration system.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Codex의 설정은 단일 파일이 아니다. 7층 레이어 모델이 운영되고, 각 레이어는 TOML 형식으로 재귀 병합된다. 레이어 간 우선순위 충돌을 해결하는 방식, per-key origin 추적, 그리고 requirements에 의한 정책 제약이 핵심이다.</p>
<hr>
<h2>1. 레이어 아키텍처</h2>
<p>레이어 우선순위(아래로 갈수록 높음):</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>System</strong> — <code>/etc/codex/config.toml</code> (Unix) 또는 <code>%ProgramData%\OpenAI\Codex\config.toml</code> (Windows)</li>
<li><strong>User</strong> — <code>$CODEX_HOME/config.toml</code></li>
<li><strong>Project</strong> — CWD 기준 <code>.codex/config.toml</code> (tree walk + repo root, untrusted 시 disabled)</li>
<li><strong>Session flags</strong> — CLI <code>--config</code> 오버라이드 (dotted-path TOML write)</li>
<li><strong>Legacy managed config</strong> — <code>managed_config.toml</code> (파일 또는 MDM)</li>
<li><strong>MDM managed config</strong> — macOS managed preferences (<code>defaults</code> 기반, macOS only)</li>
</ol>
<p>별도로 <strong>Requirements</strong> 채널이 config 레이어와 독립적으로 작동한다:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Cloud requirements</strong> — 원격 관리형 제약</li>
<li><strong>MDM requirements</strong> — macOS managed preferences 기반 제약</li>
<li><strong>System requirements</strong> — <code>/etc/codex/requirements.toml</code></li>
</ul>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph "Config Layers (낮→높 우선순위)"
        SYS["① System config.toml"]
        USR["② User config.toml"]
        PRJ["③ Project .codex/config.toml"]
        CLI["④ Session flags (CLI --config)"]
        MCF["⑤ Legacy managed_config.toml"]
        MDM["⑥ MDM managed config (macOS)"]
    end

    subgraph "Requirements (정책 제약)"
        CRQ["Cloud requirements"]
        MRQ["MDM requirements"]
        SRQ["System requirements.toml"]
    end

    SYS --> STACK["ConfigLayerStack"]
    USR --> STACK
    PRJ --> STACK
    CLI --> STACK
    MCF --> STACK
    MDM --> STACK

    CRQ --> REQ["ConfigRequirements"]
    MRQ --> REQ
    SRQ --> REQ

    STACK --> MERGE["effective_config()\nTOML 재귀 병합"]
    STACK --> ORIG["per-key origins()"]
    REQ --> STACK

    MERGE --> CFG["Config 최종 구조체"]
    REQ --> CFG
</code></pre>
<h3>1.1 레이어 로딩 흐름</h3>
<p><code>config_loader/mod.rs</code>의 <code>load_config_layers_state()</code>가 전체 레이어 스택을 조립한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config_loader/mod.rs — 진입점
pub async fn load_config_layers_state(
    codex_home: &#x26;Path,
    cwd: Option&#x3C;AbsolutePathBuf>,
    cli_overrides: &#x26;[(String, TomlValue)],
    overrides: LoaderOverrides,
    cloud_requirements: CloudRequirementsLoader,
) -> io::Result&#x3C;ConfigLayerStack> {
    // 1) requirements 수집: cloud → MDM → system requirements.toml
    let mut config_requirements_toml = ConfigRequirementsWithSources::default();
    if let Some(requirements) = cloud_requirements.get().await...? {
        config_requirements_toml.merge_unset_fields(
            RequirementSource::CloudRequirements, requirements);
    }
    // macOS MDM requirements
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    macos::load_managed_admin_requirements_toml(&#x26;mut config_requirements_toml, ...);
    // /etc/codex/requirements.toml
    load_requirements_toml(&#x26;mut config_requirements_toml, system_requirements_toml_file()?);

    // 2) config 레이어 조립: system → user → project → session flags → legacy managed
    let mut layers = Vec::&#x3C;ConfigLayerEntry>::new();
    layers.push(/* system */);
    layers.push(/* user */);
    // project 레이어: cwd에서 .codex/ 폴더를 tree-walk로 검색
    if let Some(cwd) = cwd {
        let project_layers = load_project_layers(&#x26;cwd, ...);
        layers.extend(project_layers);
    }
    if let Some(cli_overrides_layer) = cli_overrides_layer {
        layers.push(ConfigLayerEntry::new(ConfigLayerSource::SessionFlags, ...));
    }
    // legacy managed_config.toml (파일 + MDM)
    ...

    // 3) ConfigLayerStack 구성 — layers + requirements → 최종 스택
    ConfigLayerStack::new(layers, config_requirements_toml.try_into()?, ...)
}
</code></pre>
<h3>1.2 Config 최종 빌드</h3>
<p><code>ConfigBuilder</code>가 레이어 스택에서 최종 <code>Config</code> 구조체를 만든다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/mod.rs — ConfigBuilder::build()
pub async fn build(self) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;Config> {
    let config_layer_stack = load_config_layers_state(
        &#x26;codex_home, Some(cwd), &#x26;cli_overrides, loader_overrides, cloud_requirements,
    ).await?;

    // effective_config(): disabled 레이어 제외, 재귀 TOML 병합
    let merged_toml = config_layer_stack.effective_config();

    // TOML Value → ConfigToml 역직렬화
    let config_toml: ConfigToml = merged_toml.try_into()?;

    // ConfigToml + ConfigOverrides + requirements → 최종 Config
    Config::load_config_with_layer_stack(config_toml, harness_overrides, codex_home, config_layer_stack)
}
</code></pre>
<h3>1.3 Requirements 적용</h3>
<p><code>Config::load_config_with_layer_stack()</code> 내부에서 requirements가 각 필드에 적용된다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/mod.rs — load_config_with_layer_stack()
let ConfigRequirements {
    approval_policy: mut constrained_approval_policy,
    sandbox_policy: mut constrained_sandbox_policy,
    web_search_mode: mut constrained_web_search_mode,
    feature_requirements,
    mcp_servers,
    exec_policy: _,
    enforce_residency,
    network: network_requirements,
} = config_layer_stack.requirements().clone();

// 사용자 설정값이 requirement 제약에 위반되면 fallback 적용 + 경고 발행
apply_requirement_constrained_value(
    "approval_policy", configured_value,
    &#x26;mut constrained_approval_policy, &#x26;mut startup_warnings)?;
</code></pre>
<p><code>apply_requirement_constrained_value</code>는 제약 위반 시 자동으로 안전한 값으로 fallback하고 경고를 수집한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">fn apply_requirement_constrained_value&#x3C;T>(
    field_name: &#x26;'static str,
    configured_value: T,
    constrained_value: &#x26;mut ConstrainedWithSource&#x3C;T>,
    startup_warnings: &#x26;mut Vec&#x3C;String>,
) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;()> {
    if let Err(err) = constrained_value.set(configured_value) {
        let fallback_value = constrained_value.get().clone();
        startup_warnings.push(format!(
            "Configured value for `{field_name}` is disallowed by requirements; \
             falling back to required value {fallback_value:?}. Details: {err}"
        ));
        constrained_value.set(fallback_value)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>2. 퍼미션 시스템</h2>
<p><code>config/permissions.rs</code>가 TOML 기반 퍼미션 프로파일을 컴파일한다. 파일시스템과 네트워크 두 축으로 샌드박스 정책을 생성한다.</p>
<h3>2.1 퍼미션 TOML 구조</h3>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/permissions.rs — 핵심 타입
pub struct PermissionsToml {
    pub entries: BTreeMap&#x3C;String, PermissionProfileToml>,  // 이름별 프로파일
}

pub struct PermissionProfileToml {
    pub filesystem: Option&#x3C;FilesystemPermissionsToml>,  // 파일시스템 규칙
    pub network: Option&#x3C;NetworkToml>,                    // 네트워크 규칙
}

// 파일시스템 경로별 접근 권한
pub enum FilesystemPermissionToml {
    Access(FileSystemAccessMode),                        // 단순: read/write/read_write
    Scoped(BTreeMap&#x3C;String, FileSystemAccessMode>),      // 하위경로별 세분화
}
</code></pre>
<p>TOML 설정 예시:</p>
<pre><code class="language-toml">[permissions.strict]
[permissions.strict.filesystem]
":project_roots" = "read_write"
":tmpdir" = "read_write"
"/usr/local/bin" = "read"

[permissions.strict.network]
enabled = true
mode = "limited"
allowed_domains = ["api.openai.com", "github.com"]
</code></pre>
<h3>2.2 퍼미션 컴파일 흐름</h3>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    A["config.toml\n[permissions.NAME]"] --> B["resolve_permission_profile()"]
    B --> C["compile_permission_profile()"]
    C --> D["FileSystemSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> E["NetworkSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> W["startup_warnings"]

    subgraph "특수 경로 해석"
        S1[":root → Root"]
        S2[":minimal → Minimal"]
        S3[":project_roots → ProjectRoots"]
        S4[":tmpdir → Tmpdir"]
        S5[":unknown → Unknown + 경고"]
    end
    C --> S1
</code></pre>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/permissions.rs — 퍼미션 컴파일
pub(crate) fn compile_permission_profile(
    permissions: &#x26;PermissionsToml,
    profile_name: &#x26;str,
    startup_warnings: &#x26;mut Vec&#x3C;String>,
) -> io::Result&#x3C;(FileSystemSandboxPolicy, NetworkSandboxPolicy)> {
    let profile = resolve_permission_profile(permissions, profile_name)?;

    // 파일시스템: 각 경로를 FileSystemSandboxEntry로 컴파일
    let mut entries = Vec::new();
    if let Some(filesystem) = profile.filesystem.as_ref() {
        for (path, permission) in &#x26;filesystem.entries {
            compile_filesystem_permission(path, permission, &#x26;mut entries, startup_warnings)?;
        }
    }
    // 네트워크: enabled 여부에 따라 Enabled/Restricted
    let network_sandbox_policy = compile_network_sandbox_policy(profile.network.as_ref());

    Ok((FileSystemSandboxPolicy::restricted(entries), network_sandbox_policy))
}
</code></pre>
<h3>2.3 특수 경로와 전방 호환성</h3>
<p>미인식 <code>:special_path</code>를 <code>FileSystemSpecialPath::Unknown</code>으로 round-trip하여 이전 버전에서도 config load가 실패하지 않게 보장한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// 전방 호환 파서: 새 특수 경로 추가 시 구버전이 reject하지 않음
fn parse_special_path(path: &#x26;str) -> Option&#x3C;FileSystemSpecialPath> {
    match path {
        ":root"          => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Root),
        ":minimal"       => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Minimal),
        ":project_roots" => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::project_roots(None)),
        ":tmpdir"        => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Tmpdir),
        _ if path.starts_with(':') => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::unknown(path, None)),
        _ => None,
    }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>2.4 NetworkToml 상세</h3>
<p>네트워크 설정은 <code>NetworkProxyConfig</code>로 변환되어 프록시 수준의 네트워크 제어를 수행한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">pub struct NetworkToml {
    pub enabled: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    pub proxy_url: Option&#x3C;String>,
    pub enable_socks5: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    pub mode: Option&#x3C;NetworkMode>,               // limited | full
    pub allowed_domains: Option&#x3C;Vec&#x3C;String>>,     // 화이트리스트
    pub denied_domains: Option&#x3C;Vec&#x3C;String>>,      // 블랙리스트
    pub allow_unix_sockets: Option&#x3C;Vec&#x3C;String>>,
    pub dangerously_allow_non_loopback_proxy: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    pub dangerously_allow_all_unix_sockets: Option&#x3C;bool>,
    // ...
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>3. Feature Flag 시스템</h2>
<p><code>config/managed_features.rs</code>가 <code>ManagedFeatures</code> 래퍼를 통해 feature flag를 관리한다. requirements에 의해 pin된 feature는 사용자가 변경할 수 없다.</p>
<h3>3.1 ManagedFeatures 구조</h3>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/managed_features.rs
pub struct ManagedFeatures {
    value: ConstrainedWithSource&#x3C;Features>,
    pinned_features: BTreeMap&#x3C;Feature, bool>,  // requirements가 고정한 feature
}

impl ManagedFeatures {
    // requirements에서 pinned feature 파싱 → 후보 정규화 → 검증
    pub(crate) fn from_configured(
        configured_features: Features,
        feature_requirements: Option&#x3C;Sourced&#x3C;FeatureRequirementsToml>>,
    ) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;Self> {
        let (pinned_features, source) = match feature_requirements {
            Some(Sourced { value, source }) => (
                parse_feature_requirements(value, &#x26;source)?, Some(source)
            ),
            None => (BTreeMap::new(), None),
        };
        // pinned feature를 candidate에 강제 적용 후 의존성 정규화
        let normalized = normalize_candidate(configured_features, &#x26;pinned_features);
        validate_pinned_features(&#x26;normalized, &#x26;pinned_features, source.as_ref())?;
        Ok(Self { ... })
    }

    // 변경 시도 시 pinned feature 제약 검증
    pub fn set(&#x26;mut self, candidate: Features) -> ConstraintResult&#x3C;()> {
        let normalized = self.normalize_and_validate(candidate)?;
        self.value.value.set(normalized)
    }
}
</code></pre>
<h3>3.2 Feature 정규화 흐름</h3>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    A["사용자 features\n(config.toml)"] --> B["Features::from_sources()"]
    C["프로파일 features"] --> B
    D["CLI overrides"] --> B
    B --> E["configured_features"]
    F["requirements.toml\nfeature_requirements"] --> G["parse_feature_requirements()"]
    G --> H["pinned_features\nBTreeMap&#x3C;Feature, bool>"]
    E --> I["normalize_candidate()"]
    H --> I
    I --> J["pin 적용 + 의존성 정규화"]
    J --> K["validate_pinned_features()"]
    K --> L["ManagedFeatures"]
</code></pre>
<p>Feature는 다중 소스에서 합성된다 — global config, profile config, legacy 토글, CLI override:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/mod.rs — Feature 합성
let configured_features = Features::from_sources(
    FeatureConfigSource {
        features: cfg.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: None,
        experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch: cfg.experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch,
        experimental_use_unified_exec_tool: cfg.experimental_use_unified_exec_tool,
    },
    FeatureConfigSource {  // 프로파일 레벨 오버라이드
        features: config_profile.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: config_profile.include_apply_patch_tool,
        ...
    },
    feature_overrides,  // CLI --config 등
);
let features = ManagedFeatures::from_configured(configured_features, feature_requirements)?;
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>4. 모델 프로바이더 관리</h2>
<h3>4.1 ModelProviderInfo</h3>
<p>모델 프로바이더는 built-in 기본값 + 사용자 정의 확장으로 구성된다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// model_provider_info.rs
pub struct ModelProviderInfo {
    pub name: String,                               // 표시명
    pub base_url: Option&#x3C;String>,                   // API 엔드포인트
    pub env_key: Option&#x3C;String>,                    // API 키 환경변수
    pub wire_api: WireApi,                          // responses (chat는 제거됨)
    pub query_params: Option&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, String>>,
    pub http_headers: Option&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, String>>,
    pub env_http_headers: Option&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, String>>,
    pub request_max_retries: Option&#x3C;u64>,
    pub stream_max_retries: Option&#x3C;u64>,
    pub stream_idle_timeout_ms: Option&#x3C;u64>,
    // ...
}
</code></pre>
<p>Built-in 프로바이더:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>openai</code> — OpenAI API (기본)</li>
<li><code>ollama</code> — Ollama OSS</li>
<li><code>lmstudio</code> — LM Studio OSS</li>
</ul>
<p>사용자 정의 프로바이더는 <code>[model_providers]</code> 테이블에 추가하되 built-in ID는 오버라이드 불가:</p>
<pre><code class="language-toml"># config.toml 예시
[model_providers.my-local]
name = "My Local LLM"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
wire_api = "responses"
env_key = "MY_LOCAL_API_KEY"
</code></pre>
<h3>4.2 프로파일 시스템</h3>
<p><code>ConfigProfile</code>로 모델 + 샌드박스 + feature를 한 단위로 전환할 수 있다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/profile.rs
pub struct ConfigProfile {
    pub model: Option&#x3C;String>,
    pub model_provider: Option&#x3C;String>,
    pub approval_policy: Option&#x3C;AskForApproval>,
    pub sandbox_mode: Option&#x3C;SandboxMode>,
    pub model_reasoning_effort: Option&#x3C;ReasoningEffort>,
    pub model_reasoning_summary: Option&#x3C;ReasoningSummary>,
    pub model_verbosity: Option&#x3C;Verbosity>,
    pub personality: Option&#x3C;Personality>,
    pub features: Option&#x3C;FeaturesToml>,
    pub tools: Option&#x3C;ToolsToml>,
    pub web_search: Option&#x3C;WebSearchMode>,
    // ...
}
</code></pre>
<pre><code class="language-toml"># config.toml — 프로파일 전환 예시
profile = "fast"

[profiles.fast]
model = "gpt-4.1-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
sandbox_mode = "write"

[profiles.careful]
model = "o3"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
approval_policy = "unless-allow-listed"
sandbox_mode = "locked"
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>5. Config 편집 시스템</h2>
<p><code>config/edit.rs</code>의 <code>ConfigEdit</code> enum이 설정 변경의 원자적 단위를 정의한다. <code>ConfigEditsBuilder</code>가 <code>config.toml</code>을 atomic write로 갱신한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/edit.rs — 지원되는 편집 연산
pub enum ConfigEdit {
    SetModel { model: Option&#x3C;String>, effort: Option&#x3C;ReasoningEffort> },
    SetServiceTier { service_tier: Option&#x3C;ServiceTier> },
    SetModelPersonality { personality: Option&#x3C;Personality> },
    ReplaceMcpServers(BTreeMap&#x3C;String, McpServerConfig>),
    SetSkillConfig { path: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    SetProjectTrustLevel { path: PathBuf, level: TrustLevel },
    SetPath { segments: Vec&#x3C;String>, value: TomlItem },     // 임의 dotted-path 설정
    ClearPath { segments: Vec&#x3C;String> },                     // 임의 dotted-path 삭제
    // 각종 notice/flag 토글...
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>6. Agent 역할 시스템</h2>
<p><code>config/agent_roles.rs</code>가 레이어별 agent 역할 정의를 병합한다. 역할은 TOML 선언 + <code>agents/</code> 디렉토리 자동 발견 두 경로로 로딩된다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/agent_roles.rs — 레이어별 역할 병합
pub(crate) fn load_agent_roles(
    cfg: &#x26;ConfigToml,
    config_layer_stack: &#x26;ConfigLayerStack,
    startup_warnings: &#x26;mut Vec&#x3C;String>,
) -> std::io::Result&#x3C;BTreeMap&#x3C;String, AgentRoleConfig>> {
    // 낮은 우선순위 레이어부터 순회하며 역할 수집
    let layers = config_layer_stack.get_layers(
        ConfigLayerStackOrdering::LowestPrecedenceFirst, false);
    let mut roles: BTreeMap&#x3C;String, AgentRoleConfig> = BTreeMap::new();
    for layer in layers {
        // 1) [agents.roles] 선언 파싱
        // 2) agents/ 디렉토리에서 .toml 파일 자동 발견
        // 3) 같은 이름 → 상위 레이어가 하위 필드 merge
    }
    Ok(roles)
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>7. ConfigService — 런타임 설정 API</h2>
<p><code>config/service.rs</code>의 <code>ConfigService</code>가 app-server를 통해 설정의 CRUD를 제공한다. 레이어 메타데이터 조회, per-key override 상태, batch write를 지원한다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph LR
    APP["App Server / TUI"] --> SVC["ConfigService"]
    SVC --> READ["ConfigReadParams\n레이어 메타 + per-key origin"]
    SVC --> WRITE["ConfigValueWriteParams\ndotted-path 단일 쓰기"]
    SVC --> BATCH["ConfigBatchWriteParams\n다중 키 atomic 쓰기"]
    SVC --> TOML["config.toml\natomic file write"]
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>8. JSON Schema 생성</h2>
<p><code>config/schema.rs</code>가 <code>ConfigToml</code>에서 Draft-07 JSON Schema를 자동 생성한다. <code>features</code>와 <code>mcp_servers</code> 키는 커스텀 스키마로 처리된다:</p>
<pre><code class="language-rust">// config/schema.rs
pub fn config_schema() -> RootSchema {
    SchemaSettings::draft07()
        .with(|settings| { settings.option_add_null_type = false; })
        .into_generator()
        .into_root_schema_for::&#x3C;ConfigToml>()
}

// features 키: 알려진 feature + legacy key만 허용, additional_properties = false
pub(crate) fn features_schema(schema_gen: &#x26;mut SchemaGenerator) -> Schema {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        validation.properties.insert(feature.key.to_string(), ...);
    }
    validation.additional_properties = Some(Box::new(Schema::Bool(false)));
    ...
}
</code></pre>
<hr>
<h2>9. ConfigToml 주요 필드 맵</h2>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    subgraph "ConfigToml 주요 섹션"
        M["model / model_provider\n모델 선택"]
        P["permissions\n파일시스템·네트워크 샌드박스"]
        F["features\n기능 플래그"]
        MCP["mcp_servers\nMCP 서버 정의"]
        PRF["profiles\n명명된 프로파일"]
        AG["agents\n에이전트 역할·스레드 제한"]
        MEM["memories\n메모리 서브시스템"]
        SK["skills\n스킬 토글"]
        PL["plugins\n플러그인 설정"]
        PRJ["projects\n프로젝트별 trust_level"]
        TUI["tui\nUI 설정 (테마, 상태줄 등)"]
    end
</code></pre>
<hr>

<h3>포크 관점</h3>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> 7층 layering과 TOML merge는 재사용하기 좋다</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> per-key origin 추적은 설정 디버깅 UI에 바로 활용 가능</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>ConfigRequirements</code>는 정책 적용의 핵심이므로 exec-policy, sandbox와 함께 가져가야 한다</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>ManagedFeatures</code>의 pinned + normalize 패턴은 enterprise feature gate에 적합하다</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> <code>ConfigEdit</code> + atomic write는 concurrent 환경에서 안전한 설정 변경 패턴이다</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> permission profile + 특수 경로 시스템은 샌드박스와 밀접하게 연동된다</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> MDM 레이어는 macOS 전용이므로 포크 시 scope를 정해야 한다</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> cloud requirements(<code>cloud_requirements.rs</code>)는 OpenAI 종속이므로 제거 대상</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <code>model_provider_info.rs</code>의 built-in 프로바이더 목록은 포크 시 교체 필요</li>
</ul>
<h3>핵심 파일 목록</h3>

1d:["$","$L28","20-axis-08-config",{"id":"20-axis-08-config","order":25,"tier":"deep","title":"Axis 08: Config system","description":"008_config-system/08_co_config.md","lang":"en","badge":"AXIS","children":["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$42"}}]}]
43:Tec92,<h2>Overview</h2>
<p><strong>Axis 09: MCP orchestration</strong> — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code> — MCP orchestration.</p>
</blockquote>
</li>
<li>Codex의 MCP 레이어는 단순한 "외부 도구 연결"이 아니다. 설정 로더가 여러 config layer에서 MCP 서버 정의를 모으고, <code>McpManager</code>가 plugin이 내놓는 서버와 <code>codex_apps</code>를 다시 합치며, <code>McpConnectionManager</code>가 실제 연결과 listing과 sandbox 상태 전파를 담당한다. 그 위에서 <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code>가 승인, Guardian 연계, 결과 정규화</li>
<li>이 축이 중요한 이유는 MCP가 Codex의 확장성 대부분과 직접 닿기 때문이다. 스킬이 요구하는 MCP dependency 설치, plugin이 선언하는 MCP 서버, ChatGPT 계정에 묶인 <code>codex_apps</code>, OAuth 로그인, sandbox state 주입이 모두 이 경로를 지나간다. Phase C 검증에서 버티려면 "어디에 코드가 있다" 수준이 아니라, 설정 병합과 호출 파이프라인이 어떤 레이어에서 분리되는지까</li>
<li>기존 문서의 경로 표기 중 <code>120_codex-cli/...</code>는 실제 저장소 기준과 맞지 않는다. 이 문서는 실제 구현 경로인 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/...</code> 기준으로 다시 정리한다.</li>
<li>Codex의 MCP 설정은 한 번에 완성되지 않는다. 먼저 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs</code>와 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs</code>가 system, user, project, runtime layer를 병합해 <code>Config.mcp_servers</code>를 만든다. 이때 requirements가 있으면 `const</li>
<li>그 다음 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code>의 <code>McpManager</code>가 <code>config.mcp_servers</code>와 plugin이 노출한 MCP 서버를 합친다. 마지막으로 <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code>가 ChatGPT 인증과 <code>Feature::Apps</code>가 모두 살아 있을 때만 <code>codex_apps</code>를 주입한다. 즉, "설정에서 선언된 MCP"와 "런타임에서 추가된 MCP</li>
<li>실제 연결 lifecycle은 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>가 맡는다. 이 매니저는 각 서버별 <code>AsyncManagedClient</code>를 만들고, 시작 이벤트를 발행하고, 초기 tool snapshot과 cache를 관리하며, 서버가 실험 capability로 <code>codex/sandbox-state</code>를 광고하면 `codex/sandbox-state/u</li>
<li>도구 호출은 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs</code>에서 별도 파이프라인을 탄다. 여기서 approval 필요 여부를 판단하고, Guardian 또는 사용자 입력으로 승인 결정을 받고, 실제 호출은 <code>Session::call_tool()</code>과 <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code>로 넘긴다. timeout은 이 파일이 아니라 서버 설정의 `tool_</li>
<li>graph TD</li>

<h2>Main analysis</h2>
<h1>Codex MCP orchestration analysis</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Analysis snapshot</strong>: <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code> — MCP orchestration.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Codex's MCP layer is not just a simple "connection to external tools". The configuration loader collects MCP server definitions from multiple config layers, <code>McpManager</code> re-merges the servers released by plugins and <code>codex_apps</code>, and <code>McpConnectionManager</code> is responsible for actual connection, listing, and sandbox status propagation. Above that, <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code> covers authorization, Guardian linkage, result normalization, and telemetry.</p>
<p>The reason this axis is important is because MCP directly touches most of Codex's scalability. Installation of the MCP dependency required by the skill, MCP server declared by the plugin, <code>codex_apps</code> tied to the ChatGPT account, OAuth login, and sandbox state injection all pass through this path. To survive Phase C verification, you need to show not just “where the code is,” but also what layer the configuration merge and call pipeline are separated from.</p>
<p>Among the path notations in the existing document, <code>120_codex-cli/...</code> does not meet the actual storage standards. This document is reorganized based on the actual implementation path, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/...</code>.</p>
<h2>Overview</h2>
<p>Setting up Codex's MCP is not completed in one go. First, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs</code> and <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs</code> merge the system, user, project, and runtime layers to create <code>Config.mcp_servers</code>. At this time, if requirements exist, <code>constrain_mcp_servers()</code> and <code>filter_mcp_servers_by_requirements()</code> disable the server or fill <code>disabled_reason</code>.</p>
<p>Next, <code>McpManager</code> of <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code> combines the MCP servers exposed by <code>config.mcp_servers</code> and plugin. Finally, <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code> injects <code>codex_apps</code> only when both ChatGPT authentication and <code>Feature::Apps</code> are alive. In other words, “MCP declared in settings” and “MCP added at runtime” are in the same map, but the assembly steps are different.</p>
<p>The actual connection lifecycle is handled by <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>. This manager creates an <code>AsyncManagedClient</code> for each server, issues startup events, manages the initial tool snapshot and cache, and when the server advertises <code>codex/sandbox-state</code> as an experimental capability, it provides the sandbox status with a <code>codex/sandbox-state/update</code> request.</p>
<p>Tool calls run in a separate pipeline in <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs</code>. Here, it is determined whether approval is needed, an approval decision is received from the Guardian or user input, and the actual call is passed to <code>Session::call_tool()</code> and <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code>. The timeout is executed not in this file, but in <code>RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once()</code> by moving <code>tool_timeout_sec</code> in the server settings to <code>ManagedClient.tool_timeout</code>.</p>
<h2>Architecture</h2>
<p>If you look at Codex MCP in layers, it is as follows.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A["config_loader/mod.rs&#x3C;br/>config layers and requirements"] --> B["config/mod.rs&#x3C;br/>Config.mcp_servers"]
    P["plugins/manager.rs&#x3C;br/>active plugin MCP servers"] --> C["mcp/mod.rs&#x3C;br/>McpManager"]
    B --> C
    AU["CodexAuth and Feature Apps"] --> C
    C --> D["effective server map&#x3C;br/>config plus plugin plus codex_apps"]
    D --> E["mcp_connection_manager.rs&#x3C;br/>McpConnectionManager"]
    E --> F["rmcp-client/rmcp_client.rs&#x3C;br/>stdio or streamable HTTP client"]
    F --> G["external MCP servers"]
    E --> H["tool and resource listing"]
    E --> I["sandbox state update"]
    H --> J["mcp_tool_call.rs&#x3C;br/>approval and execution pipeline"]
    J --> K["Guardian or user input"]
    J --> L["normalized CallToolResult"]
</code></pre>
<p>The point of this structure is that <code>McpManager</code> and <code>McpConnectionManager</code> have different roles. The former deals with “how to assemble the server map,” and the latter deals with “how to connect and maintain those servers.” This separation allows plugins and <code>codex_apps</code> to be exposed to the same surface, while approval policies and tool timeouts can be handled in a common pipeline.</p>
<h2>Core components</h2>
<h3>Configuration collection and requirements constraints</h3>
<p><code>load_config_layers_state()</code> in <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs</code> creates <code>ConfigLayerStack</code> by combining multiple config layers and requirements. After that, <code>Config::load_config_with_layer_stack()</code> in <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs</code> wraps <code>cfg.mcp_servers</code> with <code>constrain_mcp_servers()</code>. Here, servers that do not meet the requirements allowlist are not deleted and remain in the <code>enabled = false</code> and <code>disabled_reason = Requirements</code> states.</p>
<p>This design is important from a documentation perspective. Even though the MCP configuration appears to be "a single <code>[mcp_servers]</code> table", the actual runtime value is not raw TOML but <code>Constrained&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, McpServerConfig>></code> with the requirements applied. Therefore, even if the fork simplifies the config layer, if the requirements-based disable reason is abandoned, operational visibility also disappears.</p>
<h3><code>McpManager</code> and server map assembly</h3>
<p>In <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code>, <code>configured_mcp_servers()</code> first gets the custom server with <code>config.mcp_servers.get().clone()</code>, then <code>plugins_manager.plugins_for_config(config).effective_mcp_servers()</code> with <code>or_insert</code>. Add: In other words, in case of same name conflict, config takes precedence over plugin.</p>
<p><code>effective_mcp_servers()</code> applies <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code> once more here. <code>codex_apps</code> is not a regular plugin server, but a Streamable HTTP server derived from <code>Config.chatgpt_base_url</code> and <code>CodexAuth</code>. Since <code>apps_enabled_for_auth()</code> in <code>features/src/lib.rs</code> requires both <code>Feature::Apps</code> and <code>CodexAuth::is_chatgpt_auth()</code>, this server is tightly coupled to the OpenAI account model.</p>
<h3>Connection lifecycle with <code>McpConnectionManager</code></h3>
<p><code>McpConnectionManager::new()</code> in <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code> selects only enabled servers and creates <code>AsyncManagedClient</code>. Each server sends a <code>Starting</code> event, performs a handshake asynchronously, and sends a <code>Ready</code> or <code>Failed</code> status back to <code>McpStartupUpdateEvent</code>. The entire result is grouped into <code>McpStartupCompleteEvent</code> at the end.</p>
<p>Connection creation varies by transport. If <code>make_rmcp_client()</code> is stdio, it calls <code>RmcpClient::new_stdio_client()</code>, and if it is streamable HTTP, it calls <code>RmcpClient::new_streamable_http_client()</code>. If there is no <code>startup_timeout_sec</code> for each server, startup timeout is corrected to the default value and goes down to <code>start_server_task()</code> and <code>RmcpClient::connect_pending_transport()</code>.</p>
<p>The interesting part is the initial display performance. <code>AsyncManagedClient</code> can pre-read the startup cache snapshot for <code>codex_apps</code>, and returns the cached snapshot first from <code>listed_tools()</code> even if the actual server is still initializing. In other words, Codex does not want to leave the tool surface empty while waiting for MCP startup.</p>
<h3>listing, namespace normalization, sandbox state injection</h3>
<p>The listing is processed in three ways within the same file. <code>list_all_tools()</code> combines the <code>listed_tools()</code> of each <code>ManagedClient</code> to create a fully qualified tool name with <code>qualify_tools()</code>. <code>list_all_resources()</code> and <code>list_all_resource_templates()</code> iterate through the pagination cursor for each server and collect until the end. If a duplicate cursor appears, it immediately stops with a warning error.</p>
<p>Normalization is especially noticeable in <code>codex_apps</code>. <code>normalize_codex_apps_tool_name()</code>, <code>normalize_codex_apps_tool_title()</code>, and <code>normalize_codex_apps_namespace()</code> remove the connector prefix and create a namespace of the type <code>mcp__codex_apps__connector_name__tool</code>. On top of that, <code>qualify_tools()</code> applies sanitize and hash-based disambiguation once more to meet the Responses API name restrictions.</p>
<p>The sandbox state is connected to the <code>SandboxState</code> structure and the <code>codex/sandbox-state</code> capability. When the server advertises this experimental capability in the initialize result, <code>ManagedClient::notify_sandbox_state_change()</code> sends a <code>codex/sandbox-state/update</code> custom request. No action is taken on unsupported servers. In other words, sandbox state injection is based on capability negotiation, not global enforcement.</p>
<h3>OAuth and authentication status calculation</h3>
<p><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/auth.rs</code> is responsible for auth status calculation and scope interpretation. <code>compute_auth_statuses()</code> runs <code>determine_streamable_http_auth_status()</code> for each server, and <code>resolve_oauth_scopes()</code> sets scope priorities in the order of explicit, configured, and discovered.</p>
<p>Actual transport and token persistence are in <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/rmcp_client.rs</code> and <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/oauth.rs</code>. <code>RmcpClient</code> first creates OAuth transport if there is an OAuth token stored in the streamable HTTP server, and reconnects with <code>reinitialize_after_session_expiry()</code> when session expiration 404 is received. Therefore, Codex's OAuth is not a "one-time login and it's over", but a long-term connection model that includes refresh before operation and session-expiry recovery.</p>
<h3>tool call approval, timeout, normalization, error handling</h3>
<p><code>handle_mcp_tool_call()</code> of <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs</code> first parses JSON arguments and searches tool annotation and connector information with <code>lookup_mcp_tool_metadata()</code>. In the case of <code>codex_apps</code>, the enable status and approval mode for each connector are read through <code>connectors::app_tool_policy()</code>.</p>
<p>Approval is gathered in <code>maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval()</code>. If it is a destructive tool or <code>read_only_hint == false</code> and <code>open_world_hint == true</code>, approval is required. Even if it is an auto approve policy, the ARC monitor can check again and return <code>AskUser</code> or <code>SteerModel</code>. If the Guardian is activated, it is sent to <code>review_approval_request()</code>, otherwise it is asked through elicitation or general user input.</p>
<p>Persistent approval only goes to the <code>codex_apps</code> connector tool. <code>persist_codex_app_tool_approval()</code> directly writes <code>apps.&#x3C;connector_id>.tools.&#x3C;tool_name>.approval_mode = "approve"</code> in <code>$CODEX_HOME/config.toml</code> and then calls <code>reload_user_config_layer()</code>. General MCP server approval remains only in session memory.</p>
<p>timeout is not calculated directly in this file. In actual timeout enforcement, <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code> passes <code>ManagedClient.tool_timeout</code> to <code>RmcpClient::call_tool()</code>, and <code>RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once()</code> wraps it in <code>tokio::time::timeout()</code>. In other words, according to the document, "MCP tool call timeout" is the responsibility of the connection manager and rmcp client, not <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code>.</p>
<p>Normalizing the results also involves two steps. First, <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code> converts the content block of the rmcp result into a JSON value and repackages it as <code>CallToolResult</code> for Codex. After that, <code>sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model()</code> replaces the image block with a text placeholder if the current model does not support image input. Errors are wrapped in the form <code>tool call error: ...</code>, and at the startup stage, auth-required, startup-timeout, and GitHub PAT special cases are converted into messages that are easy for humans to read.</p>
<h3>Connection point between plugin and <code>codex_apps</code></h3>
<p>There are two plugin combinations. <code>effective_mcp_servers()</code> of <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/plugins/manager.rs</code> exposes the MCP server of the active plugin, and <code>PluginCapabilitySummary</code> collects connector id and server name provenance. <code>AsyncManagedClient::listed_tools()</code> adds this provenance to the tool description to surface "which plugin this tool is part of".</p>
<p>The <code>codex_apps</code> binding extends to the connector surface. <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rs</code> uses <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code> separately from the general session manager to temporarily create a <code>McpConnectionManager</code> that only launches <code>codex_apps</code>, and extracts the accessible connector list and cache from it. In other words, <code>codex_apps</code> is an MCP server, but it is also the backend of the app directory and connector permission model.</p>
<h2>Data flow</h2>
<p>The startup and listing flow is as follows.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant CFG as Config loader
    participant MCP as McpManager
    participant PLG as PluginsManager
    participant AUTH as Auth manager
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    CFG->>MCP: constrained mcp_servers
    PLG->>MCP: plugin MCP servers
    AUTH->>MCP: auth for codex_apps decision
    MCP->>CONN: effective server map
    CONN->>RMCP: create per server client
    RMCP->>SRV: initialize handshake
    SRV-->>RMCP: capabilities and server info
    CONN->>SRV: sandbox state update if supported
    CONN->>SRV: list tools resources templates
    CONN-->>CFG: qualified tool map and auth statuses
</code></pre>
<p>The tool call flow is a separate pipeline separate from startup.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant MODEL as Model
    participant CALL as mcp_tool_call.rs
    participant GUARD as Guardian or user input
    participant SESS as Session
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    MODEL->>CALL: tool call request
    CALL->>CALL: parse args and lookup metadata
    CALL->>GUARD: approval check if needed
    GUARD-->>CALL: accept decline or remember
    CALL->>SESS: call_tool
    SESS->>CONN: server and tool dispatch
    CONN->>RMCP: call tool with timeout
    RMCP->>SRV: tools call request
    SRV-->>RMCP: tool result
    RMCP-->>CONN: normalized rmcp result
    CALL->>CALL: sanitize result for model input mode
    CALL-->>MODEL: CallToolResult
</code></pre>
<p>There are four turning points to look at in this flow.</p>
<ul>
<li>Configuration merge branch: config layer and requirements end up in <code>config_loader/mod.rs</code> and <code>config/mod.rs</code>, and plugin and <code>codex_apps</code> additions happen in <code>mcp/mod.rs</code>.</li>
<li>Connection branch: stdio and streamable HTTP transport branch in <code>make_rmcp_client()</code> in <code>mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>.</li>
<li>Approval branch: Guardian path and direct user input path diverge in <code>maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval()</code> in <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code>.</li>
<li>Timeout branch: startup timeout applies to handshake and initial listing, and tool timeout applies to operation at the time of call.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2>Technical Reference</h2>
<h3>Direct answers to key questions</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Question</th>
<th>Answer</th>
<th>supporting file</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Where are MCP server settings gathered and merged</td>
<td>Config layer merging is done by <code>load_config_layers_state()</code> and <code>Config::load_config_with_layer_stack()</code>, plugin MCP merge is done by <code>configured_mcp_servers()</code>, and <code>codex_apps</code> injection is done by <code>effective_mcp_servers()</code> and <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code></td>
<td><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Where does connection lifecycle, listing, auth, sandbox state injection take place</td>
<td><code>McpConnectionManager::new()</code> creates the startup lifecycle, <code>list_all_tools</code>, <code>list_all_resources</code>, and <code>list_all_resource_templates</code> are responsible for listing, auth status is provided by <code>compute_auth_statuses()</code>, and sandbox state is provided by <code>notify_sandbox_state_change()</code></td>
<td><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/auth.rs</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>What pipeline does tool call approval, timeout, normalization, and error handling go through?</td>
<td>Approval is <code>maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval()</code>, timeout is passed from <code>ManagedClient.tool_timeout</code> to <code>RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once()</code>, normalization is <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code> and <code>sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model()</code>, and error handling is through startup display helper and tool call. Wrapping is in charge</td>
<td><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/rmcp_client.rs</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>What connection does it have with plugin or codex_apps?</td>
<td>The plugin combines active plugin MCP servers and tool provenance annotation, and <code>codex_apps</code> connects connector directory, approval persistence, and accessible connector cache. <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/plugins/manager.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code>, <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rs</code></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2>Fork Perspective</h2>
<ul class="contains-task-list">
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> Separation of <code>McpManager</code> and <code>McpConnectionManager</code> is worth maintaining. The server assembly and connection lifecycle must be separated so that plugin merge and runtime cache can be changed independently.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> Session expiry recovery and OAuth refresh of <code>rmcp-client</code> are highly versatile. If it is a fork that will continue to use streamable HTTP MCP, it is worth taking.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" checked disabled> The <code>SandboxState</code> custom request is a pretty good extension. It is more portable than forced coupling because it receives status only when the server advertises capabilities.</li>
<li>Preserving <code>disabled_reason</code> based on [x] requirements is beneficial for operational debugging. Reducing it to a simple allowlist filter makes it difficult to explain why it was disabled.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <code>codex_apps</code> auto-injection is strongly tied to OpenAI and ChatGPT auth. If it is a general-purpose fork, it is better to hide it behind the feature flag or separate it into a separate adapter.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> The two-way coupling between <code>connectors.rs</code> and MCP is large. If you don't need an app directory, accessible connector cache, or approval persistence, this layer can be boldly removed.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> <code>mcp_tool_approval_templates.rs</code> and <code>consequential_tool_message_templates.json</code> are good for UX quality, but are not essential cores. If there is no app connector product line, it can be abbreviated.</li>
<li class="task-list-item"><input type="checkbox" disabled> The automatic installation of the MCP dependency of the skill relies on the premise of a first-party originator. If it is a fork for external distribution, the installation UX needs to be redesigned.</li>
</ul>

<h2>Korean corpus (reference)</h2>
<blockquote>
<p>Verbatim KO corpus <code>009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md</code> for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<h1>Codex MCP 오케스트레이션 분석</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>분석 스냅샷</strong>: <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/</code> — MCP orchestration.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Codex의 MCP 레이어는 단순한 "외부 도구 연결"이 아니다. 설정 로더가 여러 config layer에서 MCP 서버 정의를 모으고, <code>McpManager</code>가 plugin이 내놓는 서버와 <code>codex_apps</code>를 다시 합치며, <code>McpConnectionManager</code>가 실제 연결과 listing과 sandbox 상태 전파를 담당한다. 그 위에서 <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code>가 승인, Guardian 연계, 결과 정규화, telemetry까지 감싼다.</p>
<p>이 축이 중요한 이유는 MCP가 Codex의 확장성 대부분과 직접 닿기 때문이다. 스킬이 요구하는 MCP dependency 설치, plugin이 선언하는 MCP 서버, ChatGPT 계정에 묶인 <code>codex_apps</code>, OAuth 로그인, sandbox state 주입이 모두 이 경로를 지나간다. Phase C 검증에서 버티려면 "어디에 코드가 있다" 수준이 아니라, 설정 병합과 호출 파이프라인이 어떤 레이어에서 분리되는지까지 보여줘야 한다.</p>
<p>기존 문서의 경로 표기 중 <code>120_codex-cli/...</code>는 실제 저장소 기준과 맞지 않는다. 이 문서는 실제 구현 경로인 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/...</code> 기준으로 다시 정리한다.</p>
<h2>개요</h2>
<p>Codex의 MCP 설정은 한 번에 완성되지 않는다. 먼저 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs</code>와 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs</code>가 system, user, project, runtime layer를 병합해 <code>Config.mcp_servers</code>를 만든다. 이때 requirements가 있으면 <code>constrain_mcp_servers()</code>와 <code>filter_mcp_servers_by_requirements()</code>가 서버를 비활성화하거나 <code>disabled_reason</code>을 채운다.</p>
<p>그 다음 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code>의 <code>McpManager</code>가 <code>config.mcp_servers</code>와 plugin이 노출한 MCP 서버를 합친다. 마지막으로 <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code>가 ChatGPT 인증과 <code>Feature::Apps</code>가 모두 살아 있을 때만 <code>codex_apps</code>를 주입한다. 즉, "설정에서 선언된 MCP"와 "런타임에서 추가된 MCP"는 같은 맵에 들어가지만, 조립 단계는 다르다.</p>
<p>실제 연결 lifecycle은 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>가 맡는다. 이 매니저는 각 서버별 <code>AsyncManagedClient</code>를 만들고, 시작 이벤트를 발행하고, 초기 tool snapshot과 cache를 관리하며, 서버가 실험 capability로 <code>codex/sandbox-state</code>를 광고하면 <code>codex/sandbox-state/update</code> 요청으로 sandbox 상태를 넣어준다.</p>
<p>도구 호출은 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs</code>에서 별도 파이프라인을 탄다. 여기서 approval 필요 여부를 판단하고, Guardian 또는 사용자 입력으로 승인 결정을 받고, 실제 호출은 <code>Session::call_tool()</code>과 <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code>로 넘긴다. timeout은 이 파일이 아니라 서버 설정의 <code>tool_timeout_sec</code>이 <code>ManagedClient.tool_timeout</code>으로 내려가 <code>RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once()</code>에서 집행된다.</p>
<h2>아키텍처</h2>
<p>Codex MCP를 계층으로 보면 다음과 같다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">graph TD
    A["config_loader/mod.rs&#x3C;br/>config layers and requirements"] --> B["config/mod.rs&#x3C;br/>Config.mcp_servers"]
    P["plugins/manager.rs&#x3C;br/>active plugin MCP servers"] --> C["mcp/mod.rs&#x3C;br/>McpManager"]
    B --> C
    AU["CodexAuth and Feature Apps"] --> C
    C --> D["effective server map&#x3C;br/>config plus plugin plus codex_apps"]
    D --> E["mcp_connection_manager.rs&#x3C;br/>McpConnectionManager"]
    E --> F["rmcp-client/rmcp_client.rs&#x3C;br/>stdio or streamable HTTP client"]
    F --> G["external MCP servers"]
    E --> H["tool and resource listing"]
    E --> I["sandbox state update"]
    H --> J["mcp_tool_call.rs&#x3C;br/>approval and execution pipeline"]
    J --> K["Guardian or user input"]
    J --> L["normalized CallToolResult"]
</code></pre>
<p>이 구조의 포인트는 <code>McpManager</code>와 <code>McpConnectionManager</code>가 역할이 다르다는 점이다. 전자는 "서버 맵을 어떻게 조립할 것인가"를 다루고, 후자는 "그 서버들과 어떻게 연결하고 유지할 것인가"를 다룬다. 이 분리가 있기 때문에 plugin과 <code>codex_apps</code>를 같은 표면으로 노출하면서도, 승인 정책과 tool timeout은 공통 파이프라인에서 처리할 수 있다.</p>
<h2>핵심 구성요소</h2>
<h3>설정 수집과 requirements 제약</h3>
<p><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs</code>의 <code>load_config_layers_state()</code>는 여러 config layer와 requirements를 조합해 <code>ConfigLayerStack</code>을 만든다. 그 뒤 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs</code>의 <code>Config::load_config_with_layer_stack()</code>이 <code>cfg.mcp_servers</code>를 <code>constrain_mcp_servers()</code>로 감싼다. 여기서 requirements allowlist에 맞지 않는 서버는 삭제되지 않고 <code>enabled = false</code>와 <code>disabled_reason = Requirements</code> 상태로 남는다.</p>
<p>이 설계는 문서 관점에서 중요하다. MCP 설정이 "단일 <code>[mcp_servers]</code> 테이블"처럼 보여도 실제 런타임 값은 raw TOML이 아니라 requirements가 적용된 <code>Constrained&#x3C;HashMap&#x3C;String, McpServerConfig>></code>이다. 따라서 포크가 config layer를 단순화해도 requirements 기반 disable 이유를 버리면 운영 가시성이 같이 사라진다.</p>
<h3><code>McpManager</code>와 서버 맵 조립</h3>
<p><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs</code>에서 <code>configured_mcp_servers()</code>는 먼저 <code>config.mcp_servers.get().clone()</code>으로 사용자 설정 서버를 가져오고, <code>plugins_manager.plugins_for_config(config).effective_mcp_servers()</code>를 <code>or_insert</code>로 덧붙인다. 즉, 동일 이름 충돌 시 config가 plugin보다 우선한다.</p>
<p><code>effective_mcp_servers()</code>는 여기에 <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code>를 한번 더 적용한다. <code>codex_apps</code>는 일반 plugin 서버가 아니라 <code>Config.chatgpt_base_url</code>과 <code>CodexAuth</code>에서 파생된 Streamable HTTP 서버다. <code>features/src/lib.rs</code>의 <code>apps_enabled_for_auth()</code>가 <code>Feature::Apps</code>와 <code>CodexAuth::is_chatgpt_auth()</code>를 동시에 요구하므로, 이 서버는 OpenAI 계정 모델에 강하게 결합돼 있다.</p>
<h3><code>McpConnectionManager</code>와 연결 lifecycle</h3>
<p><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>의 <code>McpConnectionManager::new()</code>는 enabled 서버만 골라 <code>AsyncManagedClient</code>를 만든다. 각 서버는 <code>Starting</code> 이벤트를 보내고, 비동기로 handshake를 수행한 뒤 <code>Ready</code> 또는 <code>Failed</code> 상태를 <code>McpStartupUpdateEvent</code>로 다시 보낸다. 전체 결과는 마지막에 <code>McpStartupCompleteEvent</code>로 묶인다.</p>
<p>연결 생성은 transport별로 갈린다. <code>make_rmcp_client()</code>가 stdio면 <code>RmcpClient::new_stdio_client()</code>, streamable HTTP면 <code>RmcpClient::new_streamable_http_client()</code>를 호출한다. startup timeout은 서버별 <code>startup_timeout_sec</code>이 없으면 기본값으로 보정되어 <code>start_server_task()</code>와 <code>RmcpClient::connect_pending_transport()</code>로 내려간다.</p>
<p>흥미로운 부분은 초기 표시 성능이다. <code>AsyncManagedClient</code>는 <code>codex_apps</code>에 대해 startup cache snapshot을 미리 읽어둘 수 있고, 실제 서버가 아직 initializing 중이어도 <code>listed_tools()</code>에서 cached snapshot을 먼저 반환한다. 즉, Codex는 MCP startup을 기다리는 동안 tool surface를 비워 두지 않으려 한다.</p>
<h3>listing, namespace 정규화, sandbox state 주입</h3>
<p>listing은 같은 파일 안에서 세 갈래로 처리된다. <code>list_all_tools()</code>는 각 <code>ManagedClient</code>의 <code>listed_tools()</code>를 합쳐 <code>qualify_tools()</code>로 fully qualified tool name을 만든다. <code>list_all_resources()</code>와 <code>list_all_resource_templates()</code>는 각 서버별 pagination cursor를 순회해 끝까지 수집한다. duplicate cursor가 나오면 즉시 경고성 오류로 중단한다.</p>
<p>정규화는 특히 <code>codex_apps</code>에서 두드러진다. <code>normalize_codex_apps_tool_name()</code>, <code>normalize_codex_apps_tool_title()</code>, <code>normalize_codex_apps_namespace()</code>가 connector prefix를 벗겨낸 뒤 <code>mcp__codex_apps__connector_name__tool</code> 류의 namespace를 만든다. 그 위에 <code>qualify_tools()</code>가 Responses API 이름 제한을 맞추도록 sanitize와 hash 기반 disambiguation을 한 번 더 적용한다.</p>
<p>sandbox state는 <code>SandboxState</code> 구조체와 <code>codex/sandbox-state</code> capability로 연결된다. 서버가 initialize 결과에서 이 experimental capability를 광고하면 <code>ManagedClient::notify_sandbox_state_change()</code>가 <code>codex/sandbox-state/update</code> custom request를 보낸다. 지원하지 않는 서버에는 아무 작업도 하지 않는다. 즉, sandbox state 주입은 전역 강제가 아니라 capability 협상 기반이다.</p>
<h3>OAuth와 인증 상태 계산</h3>
<p><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/auth.rs</code>는 auth status 계산과 scope 해석을 담당한다. <code>compute_auth_statuses()</code>가 서버별로 <code>determine_streamable_http_auth_status()</code>를 돌리고, <code>resolve_oauth_scopes()</code>가 explicit, configured, discovered 순서로 scope 우선순위를 정한다.</p>
<p>실제 transport와 token persistence는 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/rmcp_client.rs</code>와 <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/oauth.rs</code>에 있다. <code>RmcpClient</code>는 streamable HTTP 서버에 저장된 OAuth token이 있으면 먼저 OAuth transport를 만들고, 세션 만료 404가 오면 <code>reinitialize_after_session_expiry()</code>로 재연결한다. 따라서 Codex의 OAuth는 "로그인 한 번 후 끝"이 아니라, operation 전 refresh와 session-expiry recovery까지 포함한 장기 연결 모델이다.</p>
<h3>tool call approval, timeout, normalization, error handling</h3>
<p><code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs</code>의 <code>handle_mcp_tool_call()</code>는 먼저 JSON arguments를 파싱하고 <code>lookup_mcp_tool_metadata()</code>로 tool annotation과 connector 정보를 조회한다. <code>codex_apps</code>인 경우 <code>connectors::app_tool_policy()</code>를 통해 connector별 enable 여부와 approval mode를 읽는다.</p>
<p>승인은 <code>maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval()</code>에 모여 있다. destructive tool이거나 <code>read_only_hint == false</code>이면서 <code>open_world_hint == true</code>면 approval이 필요하다. auto approve 정책이어도 ARC monitor가 다시 검사해 <code>AskUser</code> 또는 <code>SteerModel</code>을 반환할 수 있다. Guardian이 활성화돼 있으면 <code>review_approval_request()</code>로 보내고, 아니면 elicitation 또는 일반 user input으로 묻는다.</p>
<p>persistent approval은 오직 <code>codex_apps</code> connector tool에만 내려간다. <code>persist_codex_app_tool_approval()</code>는 <code>$CODEX_HOME/config.toml</code>의 <code>apps.&#x3C;connector_id>.tools.&#x3C;tool_name>.approval_mode = "approve"</code>를 직접 써 넣고, 이후 <code>reload_user_config_layer()</code>를 호출한다. 일반 MCP 서버 approval은 세션 메모리에만 남는다.</p>
<p>timeout은 이 파일 안에서 직접 계산하지 않는다. 실제 timeout enforcement는 <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code>이 <code>ManagedClient.tool_timeout</code>을 <code>RmcpClient::call_tool()</code>로 넘기고, <code>RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once()</code>가 <code>tokio::time::timeout()</code>으로 감싼다. 즉, 문서상 "MCP tool call timeout"은 <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code>가 아니라 connection manager와 rmcp client의 책임이다.</p>
<p>결과 정규화도 두 단계다. 먼저 <code>McpConnectionManager::call_tool()</code>가 rmcp 결과의 content block을 JSON value로 바꿔 Codex용 <code>CallToolResult</code>로 재포장한다. 그 뒤 <code>sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model()</code>가 현재 모델이 image input을 지원하지 않으면 image block을 text placeholder로 치환한다. 에러는 <code>tool call error: ...</code> 형태로 래핑되고, startup 단계에서는 auth-required, startup-timeout, GitHub PAT 특수 케이스까지 사람이 읽기 쉬운 메시지로 바뀐다.</p>
<h3>plugin과 <code>codex_apps</code>의 결합점</h3>
<p>plugin 결합은 두 군데다. <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/plugins/manager.rs</code>의 <code>effective_mcp_servers()</code>가 active plugin의 MCP 서버를 노출하고, <code>PluginCapabilitySummary</code>가 connector id와 server name provenance를 수집한다. <code>AsyncManagedClient::listed_tools()</code>는 이 provenance를 tool description에 덧붙여 "이 도구는 어느 plugin의 일부인지"를 표면에 남긴다.</p>
<p><code>codex_apps</code> 결합은 connector surface까지 이어진다. <code>121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rs</code>는 일반 session manager와 별개로 <code>with_codex_apps_mcp()</code>를 사용해 <code>codex_apps</code>만 띄운 <code>McpConnectionManager</code>를 임시로 만들고, 그 안에서 accessible connector 목록과 cache를 뽑아낸다. 즉, <code>codex_apps</code>는 MCP 서버이지만 동시에 app directory와 connector 권한 모델의 백엔드이기도 하다.</p>
<h2>데이터 흐름</h2>
<p>시작과 listing 흐름은 다음과 같다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant CFG as Config loader
    participant MCP as McpManager
    participant PLG as PluginsManager
    participant AUTH as Auth manager
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    CFG->>MCP: constrained mcp_servers
    PLG->>MCP: plugin MCP servers
    AUTH->>MCP: auth for codex_apps decision
    MCP->>CONN: effective server map
    CONN->>RMCP: create per server client
    RMCP->>SRV: initialize handshake
    SRV-->>RMCP: capabilities and server info
    CONN->>SRV: sandbox state update if supported
    CONN->>SRV: list tools resources templates
    CONN-->>CFG: qualified tool map and auth statuses
</code></pre>
<p>도구 호출 흐름은 startup과 분리된 별도 파이프라인이다.</p>
<pre><code class="language-mermaid">sequenceDiagram
    participant MODEL as Model
    participant CALL as mcp_tool_call.rs
    participant GUARD as Guardian or user input
    participant SESS as Session
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    MODEL->>CALL: tool call request
    CALL->>CALL: parse args and lookup metadata
    CALL->>GUARD: approval check if needed
    GUARD-->>CALL: accept decline or remember
    CALL->>SESS: call_tool
    SESS->>CONN: server and tool dispatch
    CONN->>RMCP: call tool with timeout
    RMCP->>SRV: tools call request
    SRV-->>RMCP: tool result
    RMCP-->>CONN: normalized rmcp result
    CALL->>CALL: sanitize result for model input mode
    CALL-->>MODEL: CallToolResult
</code></pre>
<p>이 흐름에서 봐야 할 분기점은 네 가지다.</p>
<ul>
<li>설정 병합 분기: config layer와 requirements는 <code>config_loader/mod.rs</code>와 <code>config/mod.rs</code>에서 끝나고, plugin과 <code>codex_apps</code> 추가는 <code>mcp/mod.rs</code>에서 일어난다.</li>
<li>연결 분기: stdio와 streamable HTTP transport는 <code>mcp_connection_manager.rs</code>의 <code>make_rmcp_client()</code>에서 갈린다.</li>
<li>승인 분기: Guardian 경로와 직접 user input 경로는 <code>mcp_tool_call.rs</code>의 <code>maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval()</code>에서 갈린다.</li>
<li>timeout 분기: startup timeout은 handshake와 초기 listing에, tool timeout은 호출 시점 operation에 적용된다.</li>
</ul>
<hr>

</tr>
